description_long: This object is a large, rectangular solar energy infrastructure installation, featuring multiple solar panel arrays mounted on a robust, grid-like support structure. The panels are arranged in a staggered, multi-level configuration, with some sections elevated higher than others, likely to optimize sunlight exposure and reduce shading. The frame appears to be constructed from dark metal, possibly steel or aluminum, with visible grating and ribbed surfaces for structural reinforcement. The surface of the panels is a reflective, bluish-gray, typical of photovoltaic cells, and the overall design suggests a fixed, ground-mounted system. The perimeter is bordered by a solid, dark barrier, possibly for safety or to delineate the installation area. No visible cabling or inverter housings are apparent, and the structure is expansive, likely intended for high-capacity energy generation in an urban or industrial setting.
description: A large, ground-mounted solar panel array with a staggered, multi-level configuration and a robust metal support frame. The installation features reflective bluish-gray panels and a solid dark perimeter.
description_view_0: Front view showing the layered arrangement of solar panels and the grid-like support structure, with the perimeter barrier clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the varying heights of the panel sections and the ribbed, reinforced frame along the length of the installation.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full width of the array, the staggered panel heights, and the solid back barrier.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the sloped arrangement of the panels and the robust, dark support structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 20-30 degrees, orientation appears fixed, azimuth direction not clearly discernible.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum or steel) frame and reinforced support structure.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple rows in a staggered, multi-level configuration, approximately 10-12 panels in total.
